fields.description:
While looking into YARN-5585, we have come across the need to provide a sort order different than the current entity id order. The current entity id order returns entities strictly in the lexicographical order, and as such it returns the earliest entities first. This may not be the most natural return order. A more natural return/sort order would be from the most recent entities.

To solve this, we would like to add what we call the "entity prefix" in the row key for the entity table. It is a number (long) that can be easily provided by the client on write. In the row key, it would be added before the entity id itself.

The entity prefix would be considered mandatory. On all writes (including updates) the correct entity prefix should be set by the client so that the correct row key is used. The entity prefix needs to be unique only within the scope of the application and the entity type.

For queries that return a list of entities, the prefix values will be returned along with the entity id's. Queries that specify the prefix and the id should be returned quickly using the row key. If the query omits the prefix but specifies the id (query by id), the query may be less efficient.

This JIRA should add the entity prefix to the entity API and add its handling to the schema and the write path. The read path will be addressed in YARN-5585.

comments.body: Moving over the existing patch by [~rohithsharma] from YARN-5585 as the starting point.

During the call, I was thinking we might want to have a separate JIRA to deal with the YARN-generic entities, distributed shell, and MR, but on second thought I think it might be easier to do it in one patch. Let's see how it goes...

Updated patch to reflect write path.. The patch has following changes.
# Added *idPrefix* in TimelineEntity object with default value as 0. 
# If user tries to set null for idPrefix to mess up, collector will take care of going with 0 before encoding row key.

Pending :
# need up update same behavior for FileSystemTimelineWriterImpl also.

Thanks [~rohithsharma] for the patch.

As we are setting entity ID prefix to 0 and hence will carry it always, why not change it to primitive long instead of Long. That will ward off unnecessary boxing/unboxing. Is Long version required somewhere in the code flow ?
If yes in TimelineEntity we should use Long.valueOf instead of new Long to initialize it.

Moreover, in Generic Entity reader we need to extract entity ID prefix from row key and set it back in response.
Sorry hadn't read the description. Read part will be done in YARN-5585 so we do not need to send id prefix back in response as part of this JIRA.

bq. As we are setting entity ID prefix to 0 and hence will carry it always, why not change it to primitive long instead of Long
Fair point!! Object is mandatory when we want to use in Collections. Here, I do not have strong opinion to use Long, may be I can change to primitive type long.

bq. Here, we can not use bytes as directory. I think need to ignore entityPrefix for file system storage and carry on with default sorting order.
Should be fine for this patch. We do not really maintain FS implementation as it was test only. There was some discussion regarding some implementation for just trying out ATSv2 when we merged our branch to trunk. If we enhance FS implementation as part of it, we can relook at this, then. We may decide to ignore it as well or probably pad it with zeroes (as long cant be greater than 9223372036854775807).

I will have a closer look at the patch by tomorrow so as to move this JIRA forward.

Changes related to TimelineReaderContext should be done in YARN-5585
Should a javadoc be added over setIdPrefix method to explain that it needs to be sent in each entity instead of having a normal code comment over the variable  ?
Documentation needs to be updated as well.

Thanks for the patch [~rohithsharma]!

I think one thing we need to be clear we’re on the same page is whether the user-provided id prefix should be already inverted so that they should be stored as is, or the user provides a more natural value and the storage inverts it. I think it’s fine either way, although I do think the latter is slightly more user-friendly. What do others think?

Either way, we should be *crystal clear* about this in the javadoc so that users do not forget what needs to be done. If we go with the former (the user inverts the prefix), users would need to use {{LongConverter.invertLong()}}. It may also mean we need to move the {{LongConverter}} class to where {{TimelineEntity}} is so that users can use it.

Can we add this to javadoc of {{setIdPrefix()}} so that first, setting it with a consistent value is a requirement and second, whatever approach we're going with? We should remove the comments on the private {{idPrefix}} variable and state it as javadoc on {{setIdPrefix()}}.

(EntityRowKeyPrefix.java)
- l.48: I’m not sure if this second form of the constructor is needed

(GenericEntityReader.java)
- If we’re going to separate the reader work to YARN-5585, is this change needed?

(TimelineReaderContext.java)
- if we’re going to separate the reader work to YARN-5585, is this change needed?
- I see it use {{Long}} instead of {{long}}. We should use {{long}}.

[~sjlee0]
bq.  I think it’s fine either way, although I do think the latter is slightly more user-friendly. What do others think?
Although our use case will typically be to serve it in descending order, but what if somebody wants it in ascending order ? So I was leaning towards keeping it as-is and document the case where we may want to invert it.

bq. It may also mean we need to move the LongConverter class to where TimelineEntity is so that users can use it.
Well the converter interface currently lies in hadoop-yarn-server-timelineservice. The question thus is does it belong to hadoop-yarn-api and should we move it to hadoop-yarn-api just for this ? Can't we just mention that prefix will be taken as is. And explain how you achieve descending order by adding it in documentation. We can probably give example of created time.
Or add another method in TimelineEntity which inverts and sets prefix ID. Its just one line so duplication of code shouldn't be much of an issue. 
Thoughts ?

bq. What do others think?
As a user, I would expect my data to be read as latest first when entities are written without any idPrefix. Lets say in Tez where long running AM executes multiple DAG over a period of week or month, while reading user would expect last executed DAG first rather than last week DAG execution detail. But, since we are supporting an option to sort entities, I think user himself can take decision how does he want to read it. Again, we should document it correctly. I am fine with any approach.

I think we should discuss another point that  does  idPrefix should be part of UID?

bq. I think we should discuss another point that does idPrefix should be part of UID?
It should be part of UID. That will make life easy to make single entity query in Ember UI.
But this needs to be handled in YARN-5585 as that's reader side code.

I think we can create a separate TimelineUtils class in hadoop-yarn-api or provide something in TimelineEntity to invert long, instead of moving LongConverter due to the point mentioned above. Thoughts ?
That sounds reasonable. I haven't checked, but don't we have a utils class there already? If so, we could add the method there.
[~sjlee0], we have a class TimelineServiceHelper in hadoop-yarn-api. We can probably add this invert long method there.
Yes, that sounds like a good idea. [~rohithsharma], could you include that as part of this JIRA?

I prefer the approach to provide an utility method to invert longs, and leave the default order as ascending order for entity prefix. This appears (although subjectively) to be more natural in data queries (like ORDERBY in SQL? ). 

The patch overall LGTM. One small concern is that seems like we're spreading the default value of entity prefix (0) in the code. I think it might be helpful to have a constant such as DEFAULT_ENTITY_PREFIX to help users use it?
Sure, I will include in next patch.
Updated patch addressing review comments.

bq. I think it might be helpful to have a constant such as DEFAULT_ENTITY_PREFIX to help users use it?
I missed this in latest patch attached. Would you suggest where to add this? Is it constants or TimelineEntity object itself?

Updated patch to reflect write path.. The patch has following changes.
# Added *idPrefix* in TimelineEntity object with default value as 0. 
# If user tries to set null for idPrefix to mess up, collector will take care of going with 0 before encoding row key.

Pending :
# need up update same behavior for FileSystemTimelineWriterImpl also.

Thanks [~rohithsharma] for the patch.

As we are setting entity ID prefix to 0 and hence will carry it always, why not change it to primitive long instead of Long. That will ward off unnecessary boxing/unboxing. Is Long version required somewhere in the code flow ?
If yes in TimelineEntity we should use Long.valueOf instead of new Long to initialize it.

Moreover, in Generic Entity reader we need to extract entity ID prefix from row key and set it back in response.
Sorry hadn't read the description. Read part will be done in YARN-5585 so we do not need to send id prefix back in response as part of this JIRA.

bq. As we are setting entity ID prefix to 0 and hence will carry it always, why not change it to primitive long instead of Long
Fair point!! Object is mandatory when we want to use in Collections. Here, I do not have strong opinion to use Long, may be I can change to primitive type long.

Thanks for the patch [~rohithsharma]!

I think one thing we need to be clear we’re on the same page is whether the user-provided id prefix should be already inverted so that they should be stored as is, or the user provides a more natural value and the storage inverts it. I think it’s fine either way, although I do think the latter is slightly more user-friendly. What do others think?

Either way, we should be *crystal clear* about this in the javadoc so that users do not forget what needs to be done. If we go with the former (the user inverts the prefix), users would need to use {{LongConverter.invertLong()}}. It may also mean we need to move the {{LongConverter}} class to where {{TimelineEntity}} is so that users can use it.

Can we add this to javadoc of {{setIdPrefix()}} so that first, setting it with a consistent value is a requirement and second, whatever approach we're going with? We should remove the comments on the private {{idPrefix}} variable and state it as javadoc on {{setIdPrefix()}}.

Thoughts ?

bq. What do others think?
As a user, I would expect my data to be read as latest first when entities are written without any idPrefix. Lets say in Tez where long running AM executes multiple DAG over a period of week or month, while reading user would expect last executed DAG first rather than last week DAG execution detail. But, since we are supporting an option to sort entities, I think user himself can take decision how does he want to read it. Again, we should document it correctly. I am fine with any approach.

I think we should discuss another point that  does  idPrefix should be part of UID?

I think we can create a separate TimelineUtils class in hadoop-yarn-api or provide something in TimelineEntity to invert long, instead of moving LongConverter due to the point mentioned above. Thoughts ?

[~sjlee0], we have a class TimelineServiceHelper in hadoop-yarn-api. We can probably add this invert long method there.

I prefer the approach to provide an utility method to invert longs, and leave the default order as ascending order for entity prefix. This appears (although subjectively) to be more natural in data queries (like ORDERBY in SQL? ).
